spec.title: Model registry, presets, and cost ledger

humanSummary.text: Final audit found no P0/P1 blockers for ITOTORI-010. Remaining provider ledger and dashboard drilldown items are tracked as non-blocking DAG follow-ups.

findings.title: Provider ledger hardening remains follow-up work
findings.description: The final audit accepted ITOTORI-010 for completion, but provider ledger ingestion and reconciliation still need stronger pricing metadata handling, bigint-safe micros, token invariants, timestamp ordering, enum/check parity, and benchmark fallback coverage.

evidence.detail: Final wave 11 audit recorded provider ledger hardening as a P2 follow-up with no P0/P1 completion blocker.

findings.impact: Cost reports can remain MVP-acceptable while later provider metadata, fallback, or unknown-cost edge cases risk undercounting or ambiguous ledger rows.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: Provider pricing snapshots can be ingested from provider metadata and reconciled against ledger entries without floating-point micros loss.
Ledger writes reject negative token counts and preserve deterministic timestamp ordering for usage and billing events.
Provider and model enum or check constraints stay aligned with adapter metadata and schema migrations.
Benchmark-to-ledger fixtures cover fallback providers and unknown-cost cases without silently recording false zero cost.

acceptanceCriteria: Provider pricing snapshots can be ingested from provider metadata and reconciled against ledger entries without floating-point micros loss
Ledger writes reject negative token counts and preserve deterministic timestamp ordering for usage and billing events
Provider and model enum or check constraints stay aligned with adapter metadata and schema migrations
Benchmark-to-ledger fixtures cover fallback providers and unknown-cost cases without silently recording false zero cost
auditFocus: Floating-point or integer overflow in micros math
Negative or reordered provider usage entries
Fallback and unknown-cost runs misreported as billed zero
existingDagNodeUpdate.notes: Tracked as planned DAG node ITOTORI-052 for wave 11 completion bookkeeping.

findings.title: Dashboard and API cost drilldown is still shallow
findings.description: The final audit accepted the MVP cost ledger, but follow-up dashboard and API drilldown should add project, system, and time filters, pagination, provider adapter metadata inspection, and clearer zero-versus-unknown cost display.

evidence.detail: Final wave 11 audit recorded dashboard/API cost drilldown as a P3 follow-up with no P0/P1 completion blocker.

findings.impact: Operators may need to inspect cost details manually, and unknown-cost records can be mistaken for true zero-cost records.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: Cost drilldown APIs support project, system, and time filters with deterministic pagination.
Dashboard views expose provider adapter metadata for a selected ledger row without requiring raw provider payloads.
Zero-cost and unknown-cost records render as distinct states in the API and dashboard.
Filter combinations preserve totals and pagination metadata in contract tests.

acceptanceCriteria: Cost drilldown APIs support project, system, and time filters with deterministic pagination
Dashboard views expose provider adapter metadata for a selected ledger row without requiring raw provider payloads
Zero-cost and unknown-cost records render as distinct states in the API and dashboard
Filter combinations preserve totals and pagination metadata in contract tests
auditFocus: Cost filters that change aggregate totals incorrectly
Unknown cost rendered as numeric zero
Provider metadata drilldown exposing raw provider payloads
existingDagNodeUpdate.notes: Tracked as planned DAG node ITOTORI-053 for wave 11 completion bookkeeping.
